name: ExtensionEvidenceReport_Url
title: R5: Canonical identifier for this EvidenceReport, represented as a globally unique URI (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `EvidenceReport.url` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`EvidenceReport.url` 0..1 `uri`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `EvidenceReport.url` 0..1 `uri`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `EvidenceReport.url` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `EvidenceReport` is not mapped.
Element `EvidenceReport.url` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Canonical identifier for this EvidenceReport, represented as a globally unique URI (new)
element.comment:
Element `EvidenceReport.url` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `EvidenceReport` is not mapped.
Element `EvidenceReport.url` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Can be a urn:uuid: or a urn:oid: but real http: addresses are preferred. Multiple instances may share the same URL if they have a distinct version.

element.short: Canonical identifier for this EvidenceReport, represented as a globally unique URI
element.definition: An absolute URI that is used to identify this EvidenceReport when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which an authoritative instance of this summary is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the summary is stored on different servers.
element.comment: Can be a urn:uuid: or a urn:oid: but real http: addresses are preferred. Multiple instances may share the same URL if they have a distinct version.
